pages.raw_text:
Spokane and Bellingham have
been thrown together TO some eX-
tent in the past week. Last week
the Bellinghamites heard the as
piring lawyers from Spokane crate
at the meeting of the State Bar as
ISOLATION.


This week Bellingham will hear
four speakers from Spokane, along
the line of the political upllft
movement. Mrs. P. p. Stafford ana
Mrs. Rose Moore have been ad-
dressing the labor unions there this
week in behalf of equal suffrage.
Friday r. b. Martin, president OF
the Township club, will speak be-
fore the Whatcom county chautau-
qua. and on Saturday Congressman
Miles Poindexter will be there TO
urge his candidacy for United
States senator.


Altogether Spokane oratory is
helping to tide over the dull season
in the city by the big bay.
